rxn_class_summary: Low-temperature electrochemical generation of DMSO-stabilized halogen (Br+, I+) and chalcogen (ArS+, ArSe+) cation pools that add across alkenes/dienes/allenes to give α-halo or α-chalcogeno carbonyl compounds (and cyclized N-heterocycles from tethered tosylamides) after triethylamine treatment
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of Bu4NBr, Bu4NI, ArSSAr, or ArSeSeAr at -78 °C in the presence of DMSO generates DMSO-coordinated cation pools (X+/DMSO); these electrophilic cations then react with alkenes (no further electron transfer at the electrode during C-C bond formation)

key_intermediates.name: three-membered ring bromonium/halonium ion from X+ + alkene

electron_flow: At -78 °C the anode oxidizes the halide or dichalcogenide to a cationic X+ that is stabilized by one or two DMSO molecules, forming a persistent cation pool; this electrophilic X+ then adds to an alkene to give a three-membered halonium/chalcogenonium ion, DMSO opens it (anti, back-side attack) to give a β-functionalized alkoxysulfonium ion, and triethylamine-induced Swern–Moffatt-type elimination delivers the α-halo or α-chalcogeno carbonyl product.

probe_reagents.name: methanol quench of alkoxysulfonium intermediate (E)- and (Z)-1-phenyl-1-propene (2d)
probe_reagents.observation: (E)-2d gave 5d-Br (72%) and (Z)-2d gave the diastereomer 5d'-Br (75%); anti stereochemistry indicates back-side attack of DMSO on the bromonium ion

probe_reagents.name: thermal-stability assay of X+/DMSO solutions (warming then cooling, reaction with 2b)
probe_reagents.observation: Relative stability order Br+ < I+ < PhS+ < PhSe+, correlating inversely with electronegativity of X

probe_reagents.name: Br2 used in place of electrogenerated 1-Br
probe_reagents.observation: Br2/DMSO also delivers α-bromo ketones (entry 6), supporting Br+/DMSO as the active species

probe_reagents.name: NBO/DFT analysis of three-membered ring onium ions
probe_reagents.observation: Charge on benzylic carbon increases Se < S < I < Br, rationalizing why DMSO attacks the benzylic carbon for Br but the terminal/other carbon for Se, explaining regiochemical switch between 1-Br and 1-Se with 2d

notes: Regioselectivity with unsymmetrical alkenes (e.g., 1-phenyl-1-propene 2d) inverts between Br+ (attack at benzylic C → 4d-Br) and Se+ (attack at internal C → 4d'-Se), attributed to electronegativity-driven charge distribution on the halonium/chalcogenonium intermediate (supported by NBO/DFT). 1,3-Dienes undergo selective 1,2-addition at the internal carbon (no 1,4-product), and allenes react at the terminal carbon. Tethered tosylamide alkenes (2g) and 1,6-dienes (2h) give cyclized pyrrolidines/piperidines as single diastereomers, indicating that the cation-pool intermediates also initiate alkene cyclization. The Swern–Moffatt-like final step destroys alkene stereochemistry in the carbonyl product, but the MeOH-trapping experiment preserves and reveals the anti-addition stereochemistry of the X+/DMSO step.
